generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor providing 3‑D point‑cloud streams of the vehicle environment"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARRawPointCloud
]

instance MotorStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor reporting motor speed and torque signals from the physical twin"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStateSignal
]

instance SteeringStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor reporting steering angle and rate signals from the physical twin"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringStateSignal
]

instance ServoStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor reporting servo position and feedback signals from the physical twin"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoStateSignal
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor aggregating high‑level vehicle state (e.g., odometry, battery level) from the physical twin"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleGlobalStateSignal
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction (DataTransmitted)
instance LiDARRawPointCloud : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point‑cloud stream transmitted over Wi‑Fi/UDP from the vehicle to the DT"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance MotorStateSignal : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor speed/torque signal transmitted over Wi‑Fi/UDP"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStateSensor
]

instance SteeringStateSignal : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Steering angle/rate signal transmitted over Wi‑Fi/UDP"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringStateSensor
]

instance ServoStateSignal : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Servo position feedback transmitted over Wi‑Fi/UDP"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoStateSensor
]

instance VehicleGlobalStateSignal : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle state (odometry, battery, etc.) transmitted over Wi‑Fi/UDP"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data (processed/transformed data)
instance ProcessedPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Clustered and tracked point‑cloud data after Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARRawPointCloud
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringComponent
]

instance TranslatedMotorStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor state data after unit conversion and message alignment by Data Translation FE"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStateSignal
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationComponent
]

instance TranslatedSteeringStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering state data after unit conversion and message alignment by Data Translation FE"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringStateSignal
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationComponent
]

instance TranslatedServoStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Servo state data after unit conversion and message alignment by Data Translation FE"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ServoStateSignal
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationComponent
]

instance TranslatedVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "High‑level vehicle state data after unit conversion and message alignment by Data Translation FE"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleGlobalStateSignal
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudACCProcessingComponent
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudClusteringComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component that clusters and tracks LiDAR point clouds (ROS2 network)"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataTranslationComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO‑23247 Data Translation functional entity that serialises, aligns and converts units"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance CloudACCProcessingComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud‑based ACC algorithm (lead‑vehicle detection, distance calc, desired speed) executing off‑loaded computation"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance UserInterfaceComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "UI middleware that renders monitoring dashboards and forwards operator commands"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Displays real‑time vehicle state, projected path, and object tracks; allows data view, save and report generation"
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTrackInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes ACC algorithm in the cloud and returns desired speed to the vehicle for actuation"
    DTDFVocab:provides DesiredSpeedComputationAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, and issue emergency braking remotely"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction
    DTDFVocab:provides SetDesiredSpeedAction
    DTDFVocab:provides EmergencyBrakingAction
]

// C17: Insights
instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into current vehicle speed, steering angle, motor torque and overall health"
]

instance ObjectTrackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into detected objects and their tracked trajectories from clustered point‑cloud data"
]

// C17: Actions
instance DesiredSpeedComputationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic computation of desired speed based on lead‑vehicle distance and traffic conditions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator command to enable the Adaptive Cruise Control function"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator command to set a target speed for the ACC controller"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator command to trigger immediate vehicle braking"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DTS sends control commands back to the vehicle over the same Wi‑Fi/UDP link. Commands include ACC enable/disable, desired speed set‑point, and emergency‑brake activation. These commands are generated by the Controlling/Actuation functional entities, serialized by the Data Translation component, and transmitted to the vehicle’s actuators (motor, steering servo). The vehicle returns updated state information, which is displayed in the UI to confirm execution."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT requires that the physical twin and its digital counterpart share a common notion of time; clock synchronization must achieve at least 100 ms accuracy, which is the limit imposed by the available hardware. This synchronization constraint is required for computation off‑loading between devices; stricter sub‑100 ms bounds are mandated by external safety‑critical vehicular standards but are not imposed in this implementation."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The architecture follows the ISO‑23247 reference architecture with an entity‑based decomposition. Functional Entities are realized as ROS 2 packages; each package contains one or more ROS 2 nodes that implement the FE’s behavior. The three entities communicate via ROS 2 middleware, providing data management, actuation and user interaction. Resource management is achieved by mapping each entity to dedicated compute resources (e.g., separate on‑board computers). The overall orchestration relies on ROS 2’s publish/subscribe and service mechanisms to coordinate the entities."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection consists of a ROS 2 middleware backbone for internal component messaging and a Wi‑Fi network using UDP for external communication between the physical twin and the DTS. The architecture follows a client‑server style where the vehicle acts as a data source and command receiver, while the DTS provides processing and UI services. The system is required to sustain low end‑to‑end latency to meet safety‑critical needs, though exact bandwidth or latency figures are not stated. Redundancy and security mechanisms are not detailed in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT services can be hosted on compute resources that may reside in the cloud, as the context mentions off‑loading compute to 'better‑suited equipment, e.g., in the cloud'. No specific details on storage size, scaling strategy, deployment models (container, VM, edge) or availability guarantees are provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin complies with ISO 23247 (2021), the 'Digital twin framework for manufacturing' standard. Compliance involves adhering to Part 2 of the standard, which defines a reference architecture based on entities such as the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), the Data Collection and Device Control Entity, the Digital Twin Entity, and the User Entity. Each entity is composed of Functional Entities that perform specific tasks (e.g., actuation, data transformation, user interfacing). The standard does not prescribe specific implementation technologies, but the architecture may be realized with protocols such as OPC Unified Architecture. No additional certification or interoperability specifications are described in the document."
]
